taxonomy: Apochloa tijucae is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses, and it belongs to the subfamily Danthonioideae. The genus Apochloa is a part of this subfamily. It is a species of grass that is endemic to Mexico. Further classification of Apochloa tijucae is as follows: kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, class Liliopsida, order Poales, family Poaceae, subfamily Danthonioideae, tribe Danthonieae, and genus Apochloa. The classification of Apochloa tijucae highlights its unique position within the plant kingdom.
